input.issue_body:
## Problem description

This is an idea proposed by @mhils in #7450 to fix filter expression inconsistencies when it comes to `re`'s case-sensitivity. At the time of writing `~u`, `~m` and `~d` use `re.IGNORECASE`  flag (see [flowfilter.py](https://github.com/mitmproxy/mitmproxy/blob/v11.0.2/mitmproxy/flowfilter.py)). Other filters like `~hq` or `~bq` don't use that which effectively translates to the following:

- both `~u example.com` and `~u EXAMPLE.COM` work exactly the same (the regex is treated as case-**insensitive**)
- `~hq Host:` and `~hq host:` are case-**sensitive** - a request with `Host: example.org` header matches to the first filter, but not to the second one

It's my gut feeling rather than data-backed fact, but in vast majority of cases case-insensitive matching is expected.

## Proposal

All relevant filter operators should use case-insensitive matching by default to make things consistent.

1. Identify for which filter operators default `re.IGNORECASE` flag makes sense and apply the change accordingly
2. Implement a global option (e.g. an env variable or config parameter) that disables the `re.IGNORECASE` flag, so case-sensitive filter expressions can be used (e.g. _"I'd like to block access to `example.com/FOO`, but leave `example.com/foo` unaffected"_)
3. Add relevant test cases to cover new behaviour

## Alternatives

Instead of global "disable case-insensitive matching" flag it'd be great to control case-sensitivity per each instance filter expression. Most likely, that'd imply a change of filter expression format which sounds like a breaking change to me.

## Additional context

- original discussion: #7450
- according to [RFC 9110](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110.html) header names are case-insensitive and should be treated as such. Nevertheless, client can send them in upper- or mixed-case
- as stated in [RFC 7540](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7540#section-8.1.2) (which describes HTTP/2): _"(...) header

diff --git a/docs/src/content/concepts-filters.md b/docs/src/content/concepts-filters.md
index 1c198bc..d92e4d4 100644
--- a/docs/src/content/concepts-filters.md
+++ b/docs/src/content/concepts-filters.md
@@ -10,16 +10,20 @@ menu:
 Many commands in the mitmproxy tool make use of filter expressions. Filter
 expressions consist of the following operators:
 
 {{< readfile file="/generated/filters.html" >}}
 
-- Regexes are Python-style
-- Regexes can be specified as quoted strings
+- Regexes are Python-style.
+- Regexes can be specified as quoted strings.
+- Regexes are case-insensitive by default.[^1]
 - Header matching (~h, ~hq, ~hs) is against a string of the form "name: value".
 - Strings with no operators are matched against the request URL.
 - The default binary operator is &.
 
+[^1]: This can be disabled by setting `MITMPROXY_CASE_SENSITIVE_FILTERS=1`
+  as an environment variable.
+
 ## View flow selectors
 
 In interactive contexts, mitmproxy has a set of convenient flow selectors that
 operate on the current view:

 def only(*types):

@@ -178,11 +183,11 @@ class _Rex(_Action):

             expr = expr.encode()
         try:
-            self.re = re.compile(expr, self.flags)
+            self.re = re.compile(expr, self.flags | maybe_ignore_case)
         except Exception:

 def _check_content_type(rex, message):

-    flags = re.IGNORECASE

@@ -401,11 +404,10 @@ class FDomain(_Rex):

-    flags = re.IGNORECASE
 
     # FUrl is special, because it can be "naked".

@@ -430,11 +432,11 @@ class FSrc(_Rex):
 
     def __call__(self, f):
         if not f.client_conn or not f.client_conn.peername:
             return False
         r = f"{f.client_conn.peername[0]}:{f.client_conn.peername[1]}"
-        return f.client_conn.peername and self.re.search(r)
+        return self.re.search(r)
 
 
 class FDst(_Rex):
     code = "dst"
     help = "Match destination address"
@@ -442,11 +444,11 @@ class FDst(_Rex):
 
     def __call__(self, f):
         if not f.server_conn or not f.server_conn.address:
             return False
         r = f"{f.server_conn.address[0]}:{f.server_conn.address[1]}"
-        return f.server_conn.address and self.re.search(r)

 class FReplay(_Action):

input.issue_title: Help window doesn't appear, when changing cofing
input.issue_body: #### Problem Description While changing parameters in options (like allow_hosts or block_list), there is hint on the bottom of the window "No values - you should add some. Press `?` for help." But after I'm pressing `?` - nothing happend. No help window appear. Then when I'm quit parameter editing, help window for options appear (not for parameter changing window). Looks like something went wrog, because I'm expext help window with hotkeys for adding new hosts. #### Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Type `mitmproxy` in your terminal 2. Press `O` 3. Select `allow_hosts` and press `Enter` 4. Press `?` **Expected**: Help window appear for `alliow_hosts` window **Actual**: Nothing happend, help window for `Options` window appear after quit `allow_hosts` window. #### System Information Mitmproxy: 8.1.1 Python: 3.10.5 OpenSSL: OpenSSL 1.1.1q 5 Jul 2022 Platform: macOS-12.4-arm64-arm-64bit

input.pr_body:
#### Description

According to the Browser/CA Forum guidelines, as of March 15, 2026, certificate validity can no longer exceed 200 days.
This is implemented in Chromium with [this line](https://chromium.googlesource.com/chromium/src/+/961e21793515436af0f02a7fe4889c79249909cf/net/cert/cert_verify_proc.cc#827).
Reducing the validity from 365 days to 199 days avoids Chromium complaining about `net::ERR_CERT_VALIDITY_TOO_LONG`

#### Checklist

 - [X] I have updated tests where applicable.
 - [x] I have added an entry to the CHANGELOG.

   ([#8095](https://github.com/mitmproxy/mitmproxy/pull/8095), @seroperson)
 - Removed several dead functions using [Skylos](https://github.com/duriantaco/skylos).
   ([#8136](https://github.com/mitmproxy/mitmproxy/pull/8136), @duriantaco)
+- Reduce `CERT_EXPIRY` to 199 days.
+  ([#8142](https://github.com/mitmproxy/mitmproxy/pull/8142), @opstic)
 
 ## 24 November 2025: mitmproxy 12.2.1
 
diff --git a/mitmproxy/certs.py b/mitmproxy/certs.py
index 53c3440375..2f421e25e1 100644
--- a/mitmproxy/certs.py
+++ b/mitmproxy/certs.py
@@ -36,7 +36,7 @@
 
 # Default expiry must not be too long: https://github.com/mitmproxy/mitmproxy/issues/815
 CA_EXPIRY = datetime.timedelta(days=10 * 365)
-CERT_EXPIRY = datetime.timedelta(days=365)
+CERT_EXPIRY = datetime.timedelta(days=199)
 CRL_EXPIRY = datetime.timedelta(days=7)
 
 # Generated with "openssl dhparam". It's too slow to generate this on startup.

input.pr_title: cleanup: removed unused functions/classes and scripts
input.pr_body:
Hihi, was using this over the weekend, got a lil bit bored and did some cleanups.. 

Removed these things: 

- `setbit()`/`getbit()` in `mitmproxy/utils/bits.py`: entire file deleted, zero callers
- `SearchError` in `mitmproxy/tools/console/flowview.py`: exception never raised
- `colorize_url()` in `mitmproxy/tools/console/common.py`: zero callers
- `fcol()` in `mitmproxy/tools/console/options.py`: zero callers (diff from the used `fcol` in `common.py`)
- `view_orders` in `mitmproxy/tools/console/consoleaddons.py`: no references

Also a question: `save_settings()` in `tools/web/static_viewer.py` was added in a while back but never wired into `export()`. Not quite sure if this was intentional or should it be called there? Reverted it it in second commit, so not doing anything to that for now. 

Found with [Skylos](https://github.com/duriantaco/skylos), a dead code detection tool.

- [ ] I have updated tests where applicable.                                                        
- [ ] I have added an entry to the CHANGELOG. 

Not sure if these 2 are required for code cleanup. I did a grep for the checks.

input.pr_body:
Updates the requirements on [flask](https://github.com/pallets/flask) to permit the latest version.
Release notes

Sourced from flask's releases.

3.1.3

This is the Flask 3.1.3 security fix release, which fixes a security issue but does not otherwise change behavior and should not result in breaking changes compared to the latest feature release.

PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/Flask/3.1.3/ Changes: https://flask.palletsprojects.com/page/changes/#version-3-1-3

  • The session is marked as accessed for operations that only access the keys but not the values, such as in and len. GHSA-68rp-wp8r-4726
Changelog

Sourced from flask's changelog.

Version 3.1.3

Released 2026-02-18

  • The session is marked as accessed for operations that only access the keys but not the values, such as in and len. :ghsa:68rp-wp8r-4726

Version 3.1.2

Released 2025-08-19

  • stream_with_context does not fail inside async views. :issue:5774
  • When using follow_redirects in the test client, the final state of session is correct. :issue:5786
  • Relax type hint for passing bytes IO to send_file. :issue:5776

Version 3.1.1

Released 2025-05-13

  • Fix signing key selection order when key rotation is enabled via SECRET_KEY_FALLBACKS.

diff --git a/pyproject.toml b/pyproject.toml
index 9e4f376916..a7d42dd041 100644
--- a/pyproject.toml
+++ b/pyproject.toml
@@ -38,7 +38,7 @@ dependencies = [
     "Brotli>=1.0,<=1.2.0",
     "certifi>=2019.9.11",  # no upper bound here to get latest CA bundle
     "cryptography>=42.0,<=46.1",  # relaxed upper bound here to get security fixes
-    "flask>=3.0,<=3.1.2",
+    "flask>=3.0,<=3.1.3",
     "h11>=0.16.0,<=0.16.0",
     "h2>=4.3.0,<=4.3.0",
     "hyperframe>=6.0,<=6.1.0",
diff --git a/uv.lock b/uv.lock
index db3e843793..eca17cb265 100644
--- a/uv.lock
+++ b/uv.lock
@@ -1045,7 +1045,7 @@ requires-dist = [
     { name = "brotli", specifier = ">=1.0,<=1.2.0" },
     { name = "certifi", specifier = ">=2019.9.11" },
     { name = "cryptography", specifier = ">=42.0,<=46.1" },
-    { name = "flask", specifier = ">=3.0,<=3.1.2" },
+    { name = "flask", specifier = ">=3.0,<=3.1.3" },
     { name = "h11", specifier = "<=0.16.0,>=0.16.0" },
     { name = "h2", specifier = "<=4.3.0,>=4.3.0" },
     { name = "hyperframe", specifier = ">=6.0,<=6.1.0" },
